docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vexy Lines for Mac & Windows | Download | Buy | Batch GUI | CLI/MCP | API | .lines format

docs.title: vexy-lines-cli
docs.text:
Command-line interface and MCP bridge for Vexy Lines.

Parse .lines files, batch export to PDF/SVG, apply styles to images and video, and connect Claude Desktop or Cursor directly to the Vexy Lines MCP API -- all from the terminal.

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text:
Without the app running:

  • Inspect .lines file metadata (caption, DPI, dimensions, layer counts)
  • Print the full layer/group/fill hierarchy
  • Extract embedded source and preview images
  • Batch extract from entire directories

With the app running:

  • Apply .lines styles to images via MCP
  • Interpolate between two styles across an image sequence
  • Process video frame-by-frame with style transfer
  • Export .lines files to PDF/SVG without dialog interaction
  • Query and manipulate the live document tree

For AI assistants:

  • Bridge Claude Desktop and Cursor to the Vexy Lines MCP server via stdio

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
# Inspect a file (no app needed)
vexy-lines info artwork.lines

# Show the layer tree
vexy-lines file-tree artwork.lines

# Export a folder to PDF (auto-launches the app)
vexy-lines export ./my-art/ --format pdf

# Apply a style to photos
vexy-lines style-transfer --style template.lines --input-dir ./photos/

# Check MCP connectivity
vexy-lines mcp-status

Running vexy-lines with no arguments launches the GUI (requires vexy-lines-run).

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Installation -- install options and extras
  • CLI Reference -- every subcommand with examples
  • Export Pipeline -- how dialog-less export works
  • API Reference -- Python API for programmatic use
  • Examples -- real-world workflows

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: The CLI is built on Python Fire, so every public method on VexyLinesCLI is also callable from Python.

docs.title: VexyLinesCLI
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_cli import VexyLinesCLI

cli = VexyLinesCLI()

docs.title: info(input, json_output=False) -> dict
docs.text:
Parse a .lines file and return metadata. No app needed.

result = cli.info("artwork.lines")
# {"caption": "My Art", "version": "4.0", "dpi": 300, ...}

docs.title: file_tree(input, json_output=False) -> str
docs.text: Print the layer/group/fill hierarchy. Returns indented text or JSON.

docs.title: extract_source(input, output=None, format=".jpg") -> dict
docs.text: Extract the embedded JPEG source image.

docs.title: extract_preview(input, output=None, format=".png") -> dict
docs.text: Extract the embedded PNG preview image.

docs.title: batch_convert(input_dir, output_dir="./output", format="png", what="preview") -> dict
docs.text: Extract images from all .lines files in a directory.

docs.title: style_transfer(style, ...) -> dict
docs.text: Apply a .lines style to images via MCP. See CLI Reference for all options.

docs.title: style_video(style, input, output="output.mp4", ...) -> dict
docs.text: Apply a style to video frames. Requires vexy-lines-run[video].

docs.title: export(input, output=None, format="pdf", ...) -> dict
docs.text: Export .lines files to PDF/SVG via the plist injection pipeline.

docs.title: mcp_status(host, port) -> dict
docs.text: Check MCP server connectivity.

docs.title: tree(host, port, json_output=False) -> dict | str
docs.text: Get the live document layer tree.

docs.title: new_document(width, height, dpi, source_image, host, port) -> dict
docs.text: Create a new document via MCP.

docs.title: open(input, host, port) -> dict
docs.text: Open a .lines file via MCP.

docs.title: add_fill(layer_id, fill_type, color, host, port) -> dict
docs.text: Add a fill to a layer via MCP.

docs.title: render(host, port) -> dict
docs.text: Trigger a full render via MCP.

docs.title: mcp_serve(host, port, no_launch=False) -> None
docs.text: Start the stdio-to-TCP MCP bridge.

docs.title: gui() -> None
docs.text: Launch the GUI (requires vexy-lines-run).

docs.title: ExportConfig
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_cli.export.config import ExportConfig

config = ExportConfig(format="pdf", timeout_multiplier=1.5, max_retries=3)
Field Type Default Description app_name str "Vexy Lines" Application name format str "pdf" "pdf" or "svg" poll_interval float 0.2 Seconds between polls wait_for_app float 20.0 App launch timeout wait_for_file float 20.0 File window timeout post_action_delay float 0.4 Pause after each action timeout_multiplier float 1.0 Scale all timeouts max_retries int 3 Retries per file

docs.title: VexyLinesExporter
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_cli.export.exporter import VexyLinesExporter

exporter = VexyLinesExporter(config, dry_run=False, force=False)
stats = exporter.export(Path("./art/"), Path("./output/"))

docs.title: ExportStats
docs.text: Tracks timing and file counts. Call stats.as_dict() for a serializable dict or stats.human_summary() for a readable string.

docs.title: AppleScriptBridge
docs.text: Wraps osascript calls for app control: quit, launch, activate, menu items, window title queries.

docs.title: WindowWatcher
docs.text: Polls window titles to detect file open and export completion.

docs.title: serve(host, port, stdin, stdout, auto_launch) -> None
docs.text:
Run the stdio-to-TCP bridge loop. Reads JSON-RPC from stdin, forwards to TCP, writes responses to stdout.

from vexy_lines_cli.mcp_server import serve

serve(host="127.0.0.1", port=47384)

docs.title: speak(text) -> None
docs.text: Announce text via macOS say command. No-op on other platforms.

docs.title: discover_lines_files(path) -> list[Path]
docs.text: Find .lines files in a path (single file or recursive directory search).

docs.title: InterruptHandler
docs.text: Context manager that catches SIGINT for graceful shutdown during batch operations.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: See CHANGELOG.md for the full release history.

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: All commands use vexy-lines <subcommand>. Add --help to any subcommand for usage details.

docs.title: Parser commands (no app required)
docs.text: These work offline -- they parse .lines XML directly.

docs.title: info
docs.text:
Show metadata for a .lines file.

vexy-lines info artwork.lines
vexy-lines info artwork.lines --json-output

Returns: caption, version, DPI, dimensions, group/layer/fill counts, embedded image flags.

docs.title: file-tree
docs.text:
Print the layer/group/fill hierarchy.

vexy-lines file-tree artwork.lines
vexy-lines file-tree artwork.lines --json-output

Output shows nesting with indentation. Hidden layers are marked [hidden]. Fills show their algorithm in brackets.

docs.title: extract-source
docs.text:
Save the embedded JPEG source image to disk.

vexy-lines extract-source artwork.lines
vexy-lines extract-source artwork.lines --output photo.jpg
vexy-lines extract-source artwork.lines --format .png

Default output: <stem>-src.jpg in the same directory.

docs.title: extract-preview
docs.text:
Save the embedded PNG preview image to disk.

vexy-lines extract-preview artwork.lines
vexy-lines extract-preview artwork.lines --output thumb.png

Default output: <stem>-preview.png in the same directory.

docs.title: batch-convert
docs.text:
Extract preview or source images from all .lines files in a directory.

vexy-lines batch-convert --input-dir ./art/ --output-dir ./thumbs/
vexy-lines batch-convert --input-dir ./art/ --what source --format jpg

Options:

Flag Default Description --input-dir (required) Directory containing .lines files --output-dir ./output Where to write images --what preview preview or source --format png Output image format

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export .lines files to PDF or SVG without save dialogs. Works on a single file or a directory (recursive).

vexy-lines export artwork.lines
vexy-lines export ./my-art/ --format svg --output ./exports/
vexy-lines export artwork.lines --dry-run
vexy-lines export ./art/ --force --timeout-multiplier 2

The pipeline: quits the app, injects export preferences into macOS defaults, relaunches, opens each file, triggers File > Export, restores original preferences.

Options:

Flag Default Description --format pdf pdf or svg --output same folder Destination file or directory --dry-run False List files without exporting --force False Re-export even if output exists --timeout-multiplier 1.0 Scale all timeouts (range 0.1--10) --max-retries 3 Retry attempts per file (range 0--10) --say-summary False Announce result via macOS text-to-speech --verbose False Enable debug logging

docs.title: style-transfer
docs.text:
Apply a .lines style to images.

# Single style across all images
vexy-lines style-transfer --style look.lines --input-dir ./frames/

# Interpolate between two styles
vexy-lines style-transfer --style start.lines --end-style end.lines \
    --input-dir ./frames/ --output-dir ./out/

# Explicit image list
vexy-lines style-transfer --style look.lines --images a.jpg b.jpg c.jpg

Options:

Flag Default Description --style (required) Source style .lines file --end-style (none) End style for interpolation --images (none) Explicit list of image paths --input-dir (none) Directory of images (jpg, jpeg, png) --output-dir ./output Where to write output --format svg svg, png, or jpg --dpi 72 Document DPI for rendering --host 127.0.0.1 MCP server address --port 47384 MCP server port

docs.title: style-video
docs.text:
Apply a style to every frame of a video.

vexy-lines style-video --style look.lines --input clip.mp4 --output result.mp4
vexy-lines style-video --style start.lines --end-style end.lines --input clip.mp4

Requires vexy-lines-run[video] for PyAV, OpenCV, and resvg.

docs.title: mcp-status
docs.text:
Check MCP server connectivity.

vexy-lines mcp-status
vexy-lines mcp-status --host 192.168.1.10 --port 47384

docs.title: tree
docs.text:
Print the live document layer tree.

vexy-lines tree
vexy-lines tree --json-output

docs.title: new-document
docs.text:
Create a new document.

vexy-lines new-document --width 210 --height 297 --dpi 300
vexy-lines new-document --source-image photo.jpg

docs.title: open
docs.text:
Open a .lines file.

vexy-lines open artwork.lines

docs.title: add-fill
docs.text:
Add a fill to a layer.

vexy-lines add-fill 42 linear --color "#ff0000"

docs.title: render
docs.text:
Trigger a full render.

vexy-lines render

All MCP commands accept --host and --port (defaults: 127.0.0.1:47384).

docs.title: mcp-serve
docs.text:
Start the stdio-to-TCP bridge for Claude Desktop / Cursor.

vexy-lines mcp-serve
vexy-lines mcp-serve --no-launch
vexy-lines-mcp   # same thing, as an installed script

docs.title: gui
docs.text:
Launch the Vexy Lines GUI (requires vexy-lines-run).

vexy-lines gui

docs.title: Global flags
docs.text: All commands support --verbose for debug logging.

docs.title: Inspect a .lines file
docs.text:
$ vexy-lines info artwork.lines
{'caption': 'Landscape', 'version': '4.0', 'dpi': 300,
 'width_mm': 210.0, 'height_mm': 148.0,
 'groups': 2, 'layers': 5, 'fills': 8,
 'has_source_image': True, 'has_preview_image': True}

JSON output for scripting:

$ vexy-lines info artwork.lines --json-output
{
  "caption": "Landscape",
  "version": "4.0",
  "dpi": 300,
  ...
}

docs.title: Browse the layer tree
docs.text:
$ vexy-lines file-tree artwork.lines
group: Background
  layer: Sky
    fill: Blue gradient [linear]
    fill: Cloud texture [scribble]
  layer: Ground
    fill: Grass [wave]
group: Foreground
  layer: Tree [hidden]
    fill: Bark [handmade]
    fill: Leaves [fractals]

docs.title: Extract embedded images
docs.text:
# Source image (the original photo)
$ vexy-lines extract-source artwork.lines
{'status': 'ok', 'output': 'artwork-src.jpg'}

# Preview image (the rendered thumbnail)
$ vexy-lines extract-preview artwork.lines
{'status': 'ok', 'output': 'artwork-preview.png'}

docs.title: Batch extract previews
docs.text:
$ vexy-lines batch-convert --input-dir ./artwork/ --output-dir ./thumbnails/ --what preview --format jpg
{'total': 42, 'successes': 40, 'failures': 2, 'output_dir': './thumbnails/'}

docs.title: Export to PDF
docs.text:
Single file:

$ vexy-lines export artwork.lines --format pdf

Entire directory:

$ vexy-lines export ./artwork/ --format svg --output ./exports/

Preview without exporting:

$ vexy-lines export ./artwork/ --dry-run

Force re-export with longer timeouts:

$ vexy-lines export ./artwork/ --force --timeout-multiplier 2.5

docs.title: Style transfer
docs.text:
Apply one style to a folder of photos:

$ vexy-lines style-transfer --style watercolor.lines --input-dir ./photos/ --output-dir ./styled/
{'total': 10, 'successes': 10, 'failures': 0, 'output_dir': './styled/'}

Interpolate between two styles across the sequence:

$ vexy-lines style-transfer \
    --style soft.lines --end-style bold.lines \
    --input-dir ./frames/ --output-dir ./animated/

The first image gets soft.lines, the last gets bold.lines, and everything in between is a linear blend.

docs.title: Style video
docs.text:
$ vexy-lines style-video --style sketch.lines --input clip.mp4 --output styled.mp4
{'status': 'ok', 'input': 'clip.mp4', 'output': 'styled.mp4',
 'width': 1920, 'height': 1080, 'fps': 30.0, 'total_frames': 150}

With style interpolation across the video:

$ vexy-lines style-video \
    --style start.lines --end-style end.lines \
    --input clip.mp4 --output morphing.mp4

docs.title: MCP status check
docs.text:
$ vexy-lines mcp-status
{'status': 'ok', 'server_info': {'width_mm': 210.0, ...}}

docs.title: MCP bridge for Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "vexy-lines": {
      "command": "vexy-lines-mcp"
    }
  }
}

Now Claude can open, edit, render, and export Vexy Lines documents directly.

docs.title: Python scripting
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_cli import VexyLinesCLI

cli = VexyLinesCLI()

# Inspect
info = cli.info("artwork.lines")
print(f"{info['caption']}: {info['layers']} layers, {info['fills']} fills")

# Export
stats = cli.export("./artwork/", format="pdf", verbose=True)
print(f"Exported {stats['success']} files in {stats['total_time']:.1f}s")

docs.title: Export Pipeline
docs.text: The vexy-lines export command exports .lines files to PDF or SVG without interacting with save dialogs. This page explains how it works under the hood.

docs.title: The problem
docs.text: Vexy Lines has no headless export mode. Exporting normally requires: open file, click File > Export, choose format, pick destination, click Save. That's fine for one file. For a hundred, you need automation.

docs.title: The solution: plist injection
docs.text: Instead of automating dialog clicks (fragile, slow), the CLI injects export settings directly into the macOS preferences domain (com.fontlab.vexy-lines) using defaults write, then triggers the menu item via AppleScript.

docs.title: 1. Discovery
docs.text: Find all .lines files to export. Input can be a single file or a directory (searched recursively). Files with existing outputs are skipped unless --force is set.

docs.title: 2. Plist injection
docs.text:
PlistManager is a context manager that:

  1. Reads the current preferences via defaults read
  2. Saves a snapshot of the original values
  3. Writes the export format and destination path via defaults write
  4. On exit (success or failure), restores the original preferences

This ensures the app's preferences are never permanently modified.

docs.title: 3. App activation
docs.text: The pipeline quits the running app (if any), waits for it to fully close, then relaunches with the injected preferences. AppleScriptBridge handles the quit/launch/wait cycle.

docs.title: 4. Per-file export loop
docs.text:
For each .lines file:

  1. Open the file via AppleScript (open command)
  2. Wait for the file window to appear (polls window title)
  3. Trigger File > Export via the menu item name ("Export PDF File" or "Export SVG File")
  4. Wait for the export to complete
  5. Optionally validate the output file exists and has non-zero size

Failed files are retried up to --max-retries times (default 3).

docs.title: 5. Cleanup
docs.text: PlistManager.__exit__ restores the original preferences. The app is left running with its original settings.

docs.title: Timeout control
docs.text:
All internal timeouts scale with --timeout-multiplier:

What Base timeout At 2.0x App launch 20s 40s File window 20s 40s Post-action delay 0.4s 0.8s Poll interval 0.2s 0.4s

Use higher multipliers on slower machines or when exporting complex files.

docs.title: Window watcher
docs.text: WindowWatcher polls the app's window titles via AppleScript to detect when a file has opened and when an export has completed. It uses a configurable poll interval and timeout.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
Each file export is wrapped in retry logic:

  • Transient failures (window didn't appear, menu item failed) are retried
  • Persistent failures are logged and counted
  • The pipeline continues to the next file on failure
  • Final stats report: processed, succeeded, skipped, failed

docs.title: Stats
docs.text:
The ExportStats dataclass tracks timing and counts:

stats = exporter.export(Path("./art/"))
print(stats.human_summary())
# "Exported 42 files (3 skipped, 1 failed) in 2m 15s"

docs.title: macOS only
docs.text:
The export pipeline depends on:

  • defaults command (macOS preferences system)
  • AppleScript (osascript) for app automation
  • The Vexy Lines macOS app

It does not work on Windows or Linux. For cross-platform export, use the MCP API directly via MCPClient.export_document().

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or newer
  • The Vexy Lines desktop app for MCP and export commands
  • macOS for the dialog-less export pipeline (uses AppleScript and defaults write)

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install vexy-lines-cli

This pulls in vexy-lines-apy (MCP client, style engine) and vexy-lines-py (parser) automatically.

Or with uv:

uv add vexy-lines-cli

docs.title: Additional extras
docs.text:
For video processing:

pip install "vexy-lines-run[video]"

For the GUI:

pip install vexy-lines-run

docs.title: Installed commands
docs.text: Command What it runs vexy-lines CLI (with args) or GUI (without args) vexy-lines-mcp MCP stdio-to-TCP bridge server

docs.title: Runtime dependencies
docs.text: Package Why vexy-lines-apy MCP client and style engine fire CLI framework (subcommand dispatch) loguru Structured debug logging

docs.title: Verify the install
docs.text: vexy-lines --help

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/vexyart/vexy-lines.git
cd vexy-lines/vexy-lines-cli
uv venv --python 3.12
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

Run tests:

uvx hatch test
